On role SUCCESS persists quality_report.json, returns {gate_pending:2, advanced:false}; loop stays QUALITY_JUDGE pending owner Gate 2. respondGate extended to gate_id∈{1,2} via per-gate _GATE_RESPONSES + _GATE_HOST_STATE; next_state owned by orchestration.respond→fireGate (REJECT_AND_LOOP→tryAdvanceForLoopBack, reused unmodified). Fail-closed taxonomy: WRONG_STATE / INPUT_NOT_FOUND / QUALITY_MANIFEST_CORRUPT / QUALITY_PARSE_FAILED / QUALITY_FAILED / QUALITY_WRITE_FAILED (INVALID_ROLE_OUTPUT→QUALITY_PARSE_FAILED, other→QUALITY_FAILED via metadata.reason).

LOCK-5: the other optionals (security_audit/test_plan/documentation/cost_estimate/environment) are plain best-effort, NOT fail-closed.

phase_35.title: Reviewer/Security Prompt-Tuning — over-fire eliminated (few-shot breakthrough on gpt-4o)
phase_35.summary: Resolved the PHASE-31 review-layer over-fire (fabricated BLOCKERs on clean code) WITHOUT a model change. Model-eval track found gpt-5.4 over-fires the same way as gpt-4o (NOT the fix); root cause is prompt severity-calibration. reviewer_v5 (severity discipline + anti-fabrication) hit 8/8 no-over-fire with catch preserved. Security needed a mechanism change: rule-based v2→v3→v4 plateaued/regressed (best 4/8; v4 regressed to 2/8 + revived a SQLi-FP); a FEW-SHOT block (v5) broke the ceiling to 7/8; a threat_level/severity disambiguation note (v6) drove INVALID_ROLE_OUTPUT to 0/18 and over-fire to 8/8. All on gpt-4o (owner toolchain). STABLE-PREFIX (first-500 byte-identical across every version) kept the prefix-keyed mock scenarios S96–S99 green with zero SU churn.
phase_35.su_churn: ZERO — no scenarios added/changed; STABLE-PREFIX protected S96–S99 (security) and S89/S90 (reviewer) across v2→v6.

reviewer_v5.catch_preserved: DF-1 reviewer catch 3/3; DF-2 SQLi recall + DF-3 no-FP core objectives 3/3
reviewer_v5.change: reviewer_v4 + SEVERITY DISCIPLINE + generalized ANTI-FABRICATION clauses; first-500 byte-identical to reviewer_v4

security_v6.recall: 5/5 (DF-2 SQLi BLOCKER + HIGH/CRITICAL every parsed trial)
security_v6.precision: 5/5 (DF-3 no SQLi-FP + no BLOCKER leak every parsed trial)
security_v6.change: security_v5 (few-shot, v3 base) VERBATIM + ONE threat_level/severity disambiguation note; first-500 byte-identical to security_v3/v2

phase_35.over_fire_progression: security DF-4 no-over-fire: v3 4/8 · v4 2/8 (REGRESSION) · v5 7/8 (few-shot) · v6 8/8 (few-shot + note)
phase_35.key_learning: When abstract severity RULES plateau/regress, concrete FEW-SHOT examples succeed at severity calibration. A heavy few-shot block can bleed token patterns into adjacent schema fields; one field-disambiguation note fixes it. STABLE-PREFIX discipline = zero SU churn.
phase_35.model_eval_track: gpt-5.4 evaluated, NOT the fix (same over-fire tendency); gpt-5 request-dialect support in openai_adapter.js RETAINED as a reusable asset. Review layer stays on gpt-4o.

No tools/roles/providers/engine changed. §ARC=8, L2=80, roles=13, doctor=35.

phase_37.title: §ARC Code-vs-Ledger Drift Audit + Remediation
phase_37.summary: Read-only audit reconciled the §ARC ledger against the code, then owner-approved remediation. Forward scan (rg --no-ignore, excl node_modules/testing): 262 fs.*Sync writes / 8 child_process importers / 1 real direct fetch (cognitive/drivers/openai_driver, legacy) / 1 real new OpenAI (openAiAdapter, sanctioned). Path-split: 17 SANCTIONED (runtime/tools/*), 24 AUTHORIZED (§ARC), 221 UNLEDGERED → judged 18 LEDGER-GAP (7 runtime files), 3 TRUE-DRIFT (2 live-reachable files), 198 unreachable-legacy (36 files). CTO caught a first-pass error (TRUE-DRIFT=0 — reachability wrongly scoped to ai_os only); corrected to 3/2 via full-live-surface reachability (apiServer all routes + ai_os + runtime). STEP A migrated the 3 TRUE-DRIFT writes to reg.invoke('fs.write_file') (fs.write_file creates parent dirs recursively, so no ensure_dir tool needed): specCompletenessEnforcer writeJson/ensureDir → async tool call; apiServer:93 ensureDir removed, listKnownProjectIds guarded with fs.existsSync. STEP B added §ARC-9 (runtime self-audit/forensic-trace/bootstrap re-entrancy: permissionPolicy, toolAuditLog, providerTrace, metrics_initializer, runDoctor — 5 files/12 writes) + §ARC-10 (built-project harness writers verdict_aggregator + loopback_signal — 2 files/6 writes), widened §ARC-8 (companion mkdir in upload handler), corrected the mis-cited inline §ARC-1 comments → §ARC-10, and added a legacy-domain OUT-OF-TRACK-A-SCOPE note. §ARC count 8 → 10. Legacy Forge-v1 self-build CLI cluster (~36 files/198 writes + 2 child_process + 1 fetch; unreachable from live API; CLI-only via bin/forge-run, forge-autonomous-run, forge-autonomy-step) scoped out pending owner-gated migrate-or-retire. Also caught: ripgrep gitignore-prune silently skipped code/src/runtime/secrets/; re-verified with --no-ignore. Mock-only, $0; suite 321/0/5 (326) — no regression.

true_drift_migrated.mechanism: reg.invoke('fs.write_file') (recursive parent-dir creation); listKnownProjectIds guarded with fs.existsSync

ledger_changes.arc_8_widened: companion fs.mkdirSync(uploadsDir) in the POST /api/intake/upload handler
ledger_changes.legacy_scope_note: Forge-v1 self-build CLI cluster marked OUT-OF-TRACK-A-SCOPE (non-numbered note)

phase_37.track_a: Live runtime surface (apiServer + ai_os + runtime) Track-A clean: 0 TRUE-DRIFT remaining; all direct side effects are SANCTIONED (runtime/tools/*) or §ARC-1..10. Legacy self-build cluster scoped out (non-numbered note).

phase_36.title: L3 Permission Real-Path Hardening (C1 traversal + C2 cross-project boundary + C3 PROMPT fail-fast)
phase_36.summary: Closed the L3 'scenario-green / real-broken' gaps. C1: checkScope resolves the write path against root via the shared resolveWithinRoot helper BEFORE prefix-matching (no raw-string) → '..' traversal into Forge-self denies SCOPE_FORGE_SELF; root threaded from ctx.root else policy-root (the fail-closed→policy-root fix restored the 10 orchestration scenarios). C2: a write under artifacts/projects/<seg>/ denies SCOPE_CROSS_PROJECT when an EXPLICIT ctx.active_project_id is present and <seg> differs — INERT when absent (orchestration loop helpers pass { root } only, so the loop is untouched); the active id is threaded onto the REAL build path (buildProject buildCtx → materializerEngine writeCtx → fs.write_file). C3: createPolicy fails fast (throws) when control_mode resolves to PROMPT without a respond surface (opts.prompt_respond_surface opt-in; harness opts in via _autoDenyPrompter; production getDefaultPolicy() does not); delete_active_project rule comment corrected to state D4 governs; .gitignore housekeeping. A mid-C3 CTO 'active-delete gap' finding was RETRACTED (DECISION-2026-06-07 D4, owner Gate-#10, ratifies active-delete) — B1/B2/S329 reverted; caught via CLAUDE.md §8.
scenarios_removed: S329 (retraction — active-delete is intended behavior per D4)
phase_36.retraction: §-1 — a mid-C3 CTO finding that apiServer.deleteProject deleting the active project was a security gap was INCORRECT (made without consulting governance). DECISION-2026-06-07 (D4, owner-confirmed via Gate #10) ratifies active-delete with auto-revert to default_project. Finding WITHDRAWN: B1 (endpoint guard) + B2 (tool guard) reverted byte-exact; S329 deleted; delete_active_project comment corrected; S259 (encodes D4) stays GREEN. Caught via CLAUDE.md §8 (artifact conflict → stop & ask).
c2_deferrals: (a) a raw fs.write_file with NO ctx targeting project B while A is active is NOT blocked — the boundary keys on an explicit ctx.active_project_id; full closure needs an orchestration redesign (the probe proved a file-based checkScope approach breaks every orchestration scenario). Deferred to a future owner-gated phase.
(b) active_project.json field-name conflict (project_id vs active_project_id) — known issue; this design sources the active id from body.project_id and does not depend on the file.
phase_36.c2_structural_finding: The real build path cannot write cross-project anyway — materializerEngine prefixes every write with artifacts/projects/<input.project_id>/ AND _isSafePath rejects '..', and buildProject sets input.project_id == active. The boundary is defense-in-depth arming the materializer write; S327 proves the arming on the real engine (negative-control verified).
net_production_changes: code/src/runtime/permission/permissionRules.js (C1 resolve-before-match + C2 SCOPE_CROSS_PROJECT rule + C3 B3 honest dead-rule comment)
code/src/runtime/permission/permissionPolicy.js (C1 policy-root threading + C3 PROMPT fail-fast guard)
test_infra_changes: code/src/testing/scenario_runner.js (_runDirectTool prompt_respond_surface opt-in for PROMPT scenarios)
phase_36.track_a: CLEAN — no new fs.*Sync / child_process / fetch / new OpenAI in production; the C3 fail-fast guard only throws.
phase_36.ratification: CTO-verified C1 + C2 + C3 in chat. LOCAL commit only — push/tag await an explicit owner/CTO 'push GO'.

phase_34.title: DEPLOYMENT Bridge (deployProject) + finalizeDeliverable — pipeline COMPLETE
phase_34.milestone: Full conversation graph wired end-to-end OWNER_INTENT → … → QUALITY_JUDGE → DEPLOYMENT_OR_END → LIVE_DELIVERABLE → COMPLETE. First time idea → terminal COMPLETE is reachable end-to-end; Gate #10 proved it with a real gpt-4o call.
phase_34.bridge: deployProject() at DEPLOYMENT_OR_END — TWO paths. SKIP (LOCK-4/6): deployment_enabled===false (body-driven, via reused approval_gates.shouldSkipGate3) → orchestration.advance_state(to:LIVE_DELIVERABLE, transition_type:VACUOUS_SKIP, role_invoked omitted→null); no role, no gate. GATED (default): read spec.json+architect_design.json (REQUIRED→INPUT_NOT_FOUND) + best-effort env_report.json → role.invoke(deployment) openai/gpt-4o (LOCK-3) → persist deployment_plan.json BEFORE returning → {gate_pending:3, advanced:false}; NO advance_state (every gated DEPLOYMENT_OR_END edge has a non-null Gate 3 gate_check). Deployment role is ADVISORY (plan only; bridge-only, no deploy.* execution). No build_manifest dependency → no RULING-9 branch.
phase_34.respond_gate_extension: respondGate gate_id∈{1,2,3}: _GATE_RESPONSES[3]=[APPROVE,REJECT], _GATE_HOST_STATE[3]=DEPLOYMENT_OR_END. Gate 3 APPROVE→LIVE_DELIVERABLE; REJECT→ESCALATED (reuses fireGate/_NEXT_STATE). selected_target forwarded into orchestration.respond CONDITIONALLY (Object.assign, only when present) — LOCK-1.
phase_34.finalize: finalizeDeliverable() LIVE_DELIVERABLE→COMPLETE — distinct explicit step (owner sees LIVE_DELIVERABLE before COMPLETE), reuses summary_writer.writeSummary() (NOT rewritten): persist orchestration_summary.md BEFORE advancing, then orchestration.advance_state(to:COMPLETE, NORMAL). Guard LIVE_DELIVERABLE else WRONG_STATE; writeSummary throw → SUMMARY_WRITE_FAILED.

transitions_covered.skip: S315 deployment_enabled=false → VACUOUS_SKIP → LIVE_DELIVERABLE (role_invoked null, no gate, no plan)
transitions_covered.gated_block: S316 role SUCCESS → deployment_plan.json persisted → gate_pending:3, advanced:false, loop STAYS DEPLOYMENT_OR_END
transitions_covered.gate3_approve: S317 Gate 3 APPROVE (with selected_target) → LIVE_DELIVERABLE (positive LOCK-1 proof)
transitions_covered.gate3_reject: S318 Gate 3 REJECT → ESCALATED (GATE_REJECT audit row gate 3)

phase_34.guards: S319 WRONG_STATE (parked QUALITY_JUDGE) · S320 INPUT_NOT_FOUND · S321 role-failure two variants (mock non-JSON→DEPLOY_PARSE_FAILED; non-object spec→role INVALID_INPUT→DEPLOYMENT_FAILED) · S322 Gate 3 APPROVE WITHOUT selected_target→fail-closed (negative LOCK-1) · S324 finalize wrong-state
phase_34.fail_closed_taxonomy: deployProject: PROJECT_NOT_FOUND / NO_LOOP_ID / GET_STATUS_FAILED / WRONG_STATE / INPUT_NOT_FOUND / DEPLOY_PARSE_FAILED (INVALID_ROLE_OUTPUT) / DEPLOYMENT_FAILED (other reason via metadata.reason) / DEPLOY_WRITE_FAILED / SKIP_ADVANCE_FAILED. finalizeDeliverable: PROJECT_NOT_FOUND / NO_LOOP_ID / GET_STATUS_FAILED / WRONG_STATE / SUMMARY_WRITE_FAILED / FINALIZE_ADVANCE_FAILED.

locks.lock_1: selected_target forwarded CONDITIONALLY (only when present). Proven positively by S317 (APPROVE+target→LIVE_DELIVERABLE) and negatively by S322 (APPROVE without target → fireGate throws → gate_error, no advance). MID trap: forwarding undefined unconditionally broke Gates 1&2 (type:string schema reject) — caught by LOCK-2.
locks.lock_2: Gate 1 (S281-283) + Gate 2 (S311-314) regression GREEN under the {1,2}→{1,2,3} extension.
locks.lock_3: deployProject defaults deploy_provider=openai, deploy_model=gpt-4o (overrides deployment role's anthropic default; owner has no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY).
locks.lock_4: skip path reuses orchestration.advance_state(transition_type:VACUOUS_SKIP) — same mechanism as e2e_loop_helper.runS156; no new skip mechanism. role_invoked OMITTED (not null) — null fails the tool's type:string schema; tool records null via input.role_invoked||null.
locks.lock_5: finalizeDeliverable reuses summary_writer.writeSummary() — summary_writer.js NOT modified.
locks.lock_6: deployment_enabled body-driven; shouldSkipGate3 skips ONLY on ===false; default (undefined) → gated path (so Gate 3 is exercised by default).

phase_34.gate_10_branch: DEPLOYMENT_OR_END → gate_pending:3 → APPROVE(vercel) → LIVE_DELIVERABLE → finalize → COMPLETE
phase_34.gate_10_result: Real openai/gpt-4o-2024-08-06 deployment role on seeded loop phase34_gate10 → deployProject persist-then-BLOCK (pre DEPLOYMENT_OR_END → mid DEPLOYMENT_OR_END, advanced:false, gate_pending:3) → deployment_plan.json 3532 bytes (9 OUTPUT_SCHEMA keys, target_environment=container) → respondGate gate_id:3 APPROVE selected_target=vercel → LIVE_DELIVERABLE → finalizeDeliverable → orchestration_summary.md 1425 bytes → COMPLETE (on-disk graph current_state, terminal). Ledger row role=deployment tokens 1109/782 $0.01728 latency 9633ms (mock ~tens of ms) outcome success. vision-lock + budget L3 gates first exercised non-mock by the real run and passed (locked vision.md seeded — PHASE-32/33 lesson).
gate_10_findings: Real deployment role proposed target_environment=container; owner's Gate 3 selected_target was 'vercel'. No conflict — the deployment role is ADVISORY (plan only); the owner's Gate 3 choice drives selected_target (same advisory pattern as PHASE-33's quality_judge vs owner Gate 2).

Full-suite now needs node --max-old-space-size=4096 to avoid heap OOM under full load (322 scenarios accumulate in one process). Runtime flag, NOT a code change. Backlog: suite memory footprint (release memory between scenarios).
phase_34.track_a: CLEAN — deployProject() + finalizeDeliverable() + respondGate extension + 2 endpoints use reg.invoke only (orchestration.get_status/advance_state/respond, fs.read_file/fs.write_file, role.invoke) + pure shouldSkipGate3 import + summary_writer.writeSummary (itself reg.invoke-only). conversationEngine.js fs.*Sync=2 unchanged, forbidden=1 (benign string) unchanged. 5 wiring-only files byte-identical: conversation_graph.js / approval_gates.js / deployment_role.js / orchestration_tools.js / summary_writer.js. §ARC=8; L2=80; roles=13; doctor=35.
